# VehiclesDB

> Open, CC-BY 4.0 vehicle taxonomy: 19,282 models across 933 makes and 6
> kinds (car, motorcycle, moped, van, truck, bus), reconciled from official
> vehicle registers of 14 countries on 4 continents. Stable ids, per-country
> availability evidence, measured popularity deciles. Versioned monthly
> (current: 2026.07.0). A model is published only when two independent
> official sources corroborate it or one shows a decisive registration count.

Use VehiclesDB to answer: does model X exist, what kind of vehicle is it,
who makes it, how is it spelled canonically, where in the world is it found,
and how popular is it there. Do NOT use it for VIN decoding, valuations,
vehicle history, or specs — it is an identity/taxonomy dataset.

Attribution: "Vehicle data by VehiclesDB (vehiclesdb.com), CC-BY 4.0."

## Data files

- [manifest.json](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vehiclesdb/vehiclesdb@latest/manifest.json): version, kinds, counts, countries, file index
- [dist/vehicles.json](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vehiclesdb/vehiclesdb@latest/dist/vehicles.json): nested make→models projection with kind, body_type, global popularity decile, availability countries
- [dist/vehicles.csv](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vehiclesdb/vehiclesdb@latest/dist/vehicles.csv): the same as a flat CSV table
- [catalog/car/models.json](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vehiclesdb/vehiclesdb@latest/catalog/car/models.json): full car records (per-country ranks, evidence, sources); same path pattern for motorcycle, moped, van, truck, bus

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/vehiclesdb/vehiclesdb/blob/main/README.md): what the data means, the Open Contract, quick starts
- [SCHEMA.md](https://github.com/vehiclesdb/vehiclesdb/blob/main/SCHEMA.md): every field, the absence rule, popularity bias notes, versioning contract
- [SOURCES.md](https://github.com/vehiclesdb/vehiclesdb/blob/main/SOURCES.md): the 14 official sources, licenses, cadences, measured gotchas
- [ATTRIBUTION.md](https://github.com/vehiclesdb/vehiclesdb/blob/main/ATTRIBUTION.md): upstream license notices

## Tools

- [vehicles Ruby gem](https://github.com/rameerez/vehicles): bundled snapshot, lookups, validators, and a built-in MCP server — `gem install vehicles` then run `vehicles-mcp` (tools: search_makes, search_models, get_model, top_models)
